Horse Legal Guide

Built for the horse world

Wise Covington

Legally sound. Financially stable.

Public index

Scenario Index

Approved scenario-based equine-law pages.

Pages

  • A boarding agreement does not mention emergency care. What now? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • A boarding barn changed facility rules mid-relationship. What should be checked? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • A boarding barn changed ownership. What happens to agreements? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • A boarding barn has unpaid invoices and unclear records. What should be organized? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • A boarding barn wants to sell a horse for unpaid bills. What matters? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • A client wants to cancel a training agreement. What terms matter? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • A clinic host and trainer disagree about responsibility. What documents matter? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • A horse died while boarded or in training. What should be documented? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • A horse was hurt while boarded. Who is responsible? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • A lessee stopped paying board or vet bills. What happens next? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • A trainer changed fees without a signed update. What should be checked? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • A trainer sold a horse without clear authority. What should be reviewed? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • A trainer wants commission after a sale. What should be checked? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • A warning sign was not posted at the barn. What risk does that create? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • I started a barn without paperwork. What risks did I create? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • Someone copied my barn name online. What should I gather? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • State warning sign rules are confusing. What should a barn review? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations
  • There is a barn name or brand dispute. What should be reviewed? — Boarding Training And Barn Operations

  • I received a demand letter about a horse dispute. What should I do first? — Demand Letters And Disputes

  • The buyer moved the horse before the dispute started. Why does that matter? — Demand Letters And Disputes

  • A horse syndicate member wants out. What documents matter? — Equine Business Formation
  • Horse business partners are disagreeing. What documents matter? — Equine Business Formation

  • A trial horse got hurt before purchase. What should be reviewed? — Horse Lease And Trial

  • A buyer skipped the pre-purchase exam and now regrets it. What matters? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • A buyer wants a refund after changing their mind. What matters? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • A buyer wants to return the horse. What matters? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • A contract names the wrong party. Why does that matter? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • A horse sale involved a minor. What extra issues can arise? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • A horse was advertised differently than described in the contract. What matters? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • A horse was misrepresented in texts before sale. What should be preserved? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • A horse was sold through a friend or agent. Who had authority? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • A horse was transported before paperwork was complete. What risk does that create? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • A payment plan horse sale has gone sideways. What should be reviewed? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • A seller disclosed an issue verbally but not in writing. What should be reviewed? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • A social media collaboration went wrong. What rights matter? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • I ignored a horse legal issue and it got worse. What now? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • I signed something I did not understand. What should I do? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • I sold a horse without a contract. What now? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • I trusted a handshake deal in a horse sale. Am I exposed? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • I used a template horse contract. Is that enough? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • The bill of sale says one thing but texts say another. What matters? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • The horse failed the pre-purchase exam after a deposit. What now? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • The seller kept possession after payment. What should be clarified? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • The seller refuses to take the horse back. What matters? — Horse Sale And Purchase
  • The seller says the horse was sold as-is. What does that usually affect? — Horse Sale And Purchase

  • A sponsor did not deliver promised payment. What should be checked? — Intellectual Property And Brand
  • A sponsor used my image without permission. What are the issues? — Intellectual Property And Brand
  • A sponsor wants exclusivity after the fact. What should be reviewed? — Intellectual Property And Brand
  • An equestrian apparel idea may copy another brand. What should be reviewed? — Intellectual Property And Brand
  • An equestrian sponsorship agreement went sideways. What should be checked? — Intellectual Property And Brand
  • A buyer claims misrepresentation after a horse sale. What matters? — Liability Waivers Insurance
  • A buyer found an old injury after purchase. What should be gathered? — Liability Waivers Insurance
  • A client posted damaging claims about a horse business. What should be preserved? — Liability Waivers Insurance
  • A horse business is using an old waiver. What should be updated? — Liability Waivers Insurance
  • A horse business owner is worried about personal liability. What should be checked? — Liability Waivers Insurance
  • A horse was injured during a paid lesson. What should be reviewed? — Liability Waivers Insurance
  • A parent signed a waiver for a minor rider. What should be checked? — Liability Waivers Insurance
  • A rider used a facility without signing a waiver. What risk does that create? — Liability Waivers Insurance
  • A rider was injured at a clinic. What should organizers review? — Liability Waivers Insurance
  • A therapeutic riding waiver is unclear. What should be checked? — Liability Waivers Insurance
  • An equine therapy client was injured. What should be documented? — Liability Waivers Insurance
  • Insurance denied a horse-related claim. What should be gathered? — Liability Waivers Insurance
  • Someone was hurt riding my horse. What should I think about? — Liability Waivers Insurance

  • A horse sale crossed state lines. What should be reviewed? — State Specific
  • A seller relied on a verbal as-is statement. What should be reviewed? — State Specific

  • A therapeutic riding program uses volunteers and minors. What should be checked? — Therapeutic Riding And Hipaa

Horse Legal Guide is an educational resource for the equestrian community.

For situation-specific legal guidance, Wise Covington PLLC is a law firm built by equestrians for the equestrian community.

The team behind Wise Covington has more than 30 years of equestrian experience.

Legally sound. Financially stable.

This content does not constitute legal advice or create an attorney-client relationship.
